summary: Problem 1 remains open in full generality: no paper has proved epsilon-flexibility for all d-degenerate graphs with (d+1)-lists for every d >= 2. Substantial partial progress exists: the d=2 case has been confirmed for planar graphs of girth >= 6 (lists of size 3) and for all graphs of maximum average degree < 3; the d=3 case has been confirmed for triangle-free planar graphs (lists of size 4); and epsilon-flexibility has been established for graphs of treewidth 2, treedepth k, and bipartite d-degenerate graphs. The general conjecture for arbitrary d-degenerate graphs with (d+1)-lists remains open for d >= 2.

since_posted.title: Flexibility of triangle-free planar graphs
since_posted.authors: Zdeněk Dvořák, Tomáš Masařík, Jan Musílek, Ondřej Pangrác
since_posted.claim: Proves that triangle-free planar graphs (which are 3-degenerate) with lists of size at least four are weighted epsilon-flexible for some constant epsilon > 0, resolving the conjecture for this class.

since_posted.title: Flexibility of planar graphs of girth at least six
since_posted.authors: Zdeněk Dvořák, Tomáš Masařík, Jan Musílek, Ondřej Pangrác
since_posted.claim: Proves that planar graphs of girth at least six (which are 2-degenerate) with lists of size three are weighted epsilon-flexible, resolving the d=2 case for this subclass.

since_posted.title: Flexible List Colorings in Graphs with Special Degeneracy Conditions
since_posted.authors: Peter Bradshaw, Tomáš Masařík, Ladislav Stacho
since_posted.claim: Proves epsilon-flexibility for graphs of treewidth 2 (which are (1/3)-flexibly 3-choosable), treedepth k graphs, and characterizes which maximum-degree-Delta graphs are epsilon-flexibly Delta-choosable.

since_posted.title: Flexible list colorings: Maximizing the number of requests satisfied
since_posted.authors: Hemanshu Kaul, Rogers Mathew, Jeffrey A. Mudrock, Michael J. Pelsmajer
since_posted.claim: Extends flexibility results to bipartite d-degenerate graphs and introduces the list flexibility number, without resolving the full conjecture for arbitrary d-degenerate graphs.

since_posted.title: Flexible list coloring of graphs with maximum average degree less than 3
since_posted.claim: Proves that every graph with maximum average degree less than 3 is epsilon-flexibly 3-choosable, resolving the d=2 case of the conjecture for a broad class of 2-degenerate graphs (including planar graphs of girth >= 6).

internal_refs_verified.note: Confirmed: proves weighted epsilon-flexibility for triangle-free planar graphs with lists of size >= 4. Abstract matches the claimed contribution exactly.
Confirmed: proves weighted epsilon-flexibility for planar graphs of girth >= 6 with lists of size 3. Abstract matches the claimed contribution exactly.

search_queries: d-degenerate graph epsilon-flexible list coloring size d+1 Dvorak Norin Postle 1612.08698
flexible list colorings special degeneracy conditions Bradshaw Masarik Stacho
flexible list coloring maximum average degree less than 3 Bi Bradshaw
notes: The conjecture splits into two sub-questions (epsilon-flexible vs. weighted epsilon-flexible); partial results address both variants. The d=1 case (forests with 2-lists) was settled in the original paper. For d=2, several major subclasses are now resolved including maximum average degree < 3 (Bi-Bradshaw 2023). For d=3, triangle-free planar graphs are settled. The full conjecture for arbitrary d-degenerate graphs remains open for d >= 2.
